title: Influencer
author: Joseph Grenny, Kerry Patterson, et al.

chapters.title: Influence makes it easier to achieve long-term goals and grow an audience
chapters.text:
Most times, the solution to the problems we have in our society today does not call for more technologies, data, or theories. Instead, we can solve our problems through our ability to influence others — a skill everyone needs to learn. As glamorous as it looks on social media, influencing others is not a game of chance. There are three things that influencers take more seriously than others:

• Focus and measure: influencers are particular about what they want to achieve and are passionate about measuring it.
• Vital behaviors: change leaders focus on essential behaviors that will assist them in achieving their desired results.
• Six sources of influence: those who seek to impact others tend to look for the different forces that shape the behavior they want to change and use them to their advantage. These forces come from six sources: personal motivation, personal ability, social motivation, social ability, structural motivation, and structural ability.

There are many influencers out there, and the good news is that what these people know and do can be learned.

Influencing is not just for social media; everyone needs to know how to facilitate change in different aspects of life. This summary provides powerful insight into making behavior changes that will last. Additionally, it presents a practical approach to leading change and empowering everyone to make a difference.

Influencers don't merely start their change efforts with their ultimate goal in mind. They take care to craft that goal into a clear and compelling goal statement. ~ Joseph Grenny et al.

chapters.title: The first key to influencing others is to focus and measure
chapters.text:
To be an influencer in any sphere, the first thing you need to do is to focus and measure. For a start, the goal you want to achieve needs to be very clear, and you should look for ways to measure your progress. Most people who desire to be influencers fail from the onset because they neglect this step. Some of the common mistakes that undermine people’s ability to influence others are:

• Vague goals
• Infrequent or no measures
• Bad measures

Vague goals

The way you define your objectives determines whether you will influence others. Having a clear and compelling goal engages the brain and hearts of others. Dr. Don Berwick, former CEO of the Institute for Healthcare Improvement (IHI), was alarmed by the fact that inadequate healthcare ranked as the sixth leading cause of death in America. To drive change, he boldly challenged thousands of healthcare professionals in December 2004 to save 100,000 lives by June 14, 2006, at exactly 9 a.m. His goal was to inspire health workers to take action and reduce deaths resulting from poor healthcare. His 100,000 Lives Campaign was so successful that he and his team beat the goal. Dr. Don Berwick achieved this result because his goals were very clear — he wanted to save lives. Another reason Dr. Berwick successfully reached this goal was giving a specific number of lives, and the goal was time-bound.

Start with a specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and time-bound goal to achieve success.

Infrequent or no measures

Even if your goals are clear and compelling, they will be unsuccessful unless you develop the proper measures to track your progress. For a measure to influence the behaviors of others, it must take place frequently.

Bad measures

It is possible to frequently take measures and still fail to undertake the ones that will help you achieve the right kind of influence, especially when you're measuring the wrong variable. So, take time to select the right variables to drive the proper behavior.

Start each change project with a strong, well-defined goal, and regularly monitor your progress. Don't guess things; track all your activities and see if you are influencing the correct behavior. Additionally, ensure you frequently take note of the right things.

Did you know? According to the University of Scranton, 92% of people who set new year's resolutions don't achieve them.

chapters.title: Change doesn’t always have to be drastic; small changes can lead to huge benefits
chapters.text:
After making clear, compelling, and measurable goals, the next thing to do is identify behaviors — anything humans do — that need to be changed to achieve the desired result. Usually, these behaviors do not need to be numerous; one or two vital behaviors are enough. For example, you could identify unhealthy eating habits as something you’d like to change with a secret recipe.

To avoid focusing all your energy on the wrong behaviors, take the time to find the specific behavior that will be useful in achieving your goal.

Here are some behavior search strategies that influencers can use to bring about change in their community:
• Notice the obvious
• Look for crucial moments
• Learn from positive deviants
• Spot culture busters

Notice the obvious

Identify behaviors that are obvious and underused. For example, many people attend meetings every day, but not everyone knows that some etiquettes allow healthy communication. These behaviors are often things you already know or were discovered by experts. Recognizing these behaviors is very beneficial in influencing others.

Look for crucial moments

While searching for vital behaviors, pay close attention to critical moments. These are times when a particular behavior puts your success at risk. Don't focus on the behaviors that bring 98% success but on the 2% that cause you to fail. Then use those 2% crucial moments to inform your actions.

Learn from positive deviants

A positive deviant is someone who, against the odds, succeeds in tough situations for reasons that seem unusual. These are people going through the same problems, but they succeed. The first thing to do is to identify these positive deviants; then need to learn from them.

Spot culture busters

As you do your vital behavior search, be on the lookout for behaviors against cultural norms. Then encourage people to speak up about those destructive behaviors. This is an excellent way to influence others.

After using the four steps discussed above to find vital behaviors, the next thing to do is research to prove the validity of the behavior. Check if an increase in behavior leads to a rise in the result you want to achieve. After identifying your results and the vital behaviors you adopted, the next thing is to look for people you will influence.

chapters.title: Personal motivation and personal ability are two crucial sources of influence that transform behavior
chapters.text:
Personal motivation means influencing others to take pleasure in what they do not particularly enjoy. Influencers have used some of these outlined techniques to help people love what they hate:

• Allow for choice: the best way to get commitment from others is by letting them know that they have the power to say no.
• Create direct experiences: the best way to help people understand the implication of their actions is to allow them to experience them. This is an excellent way to transform people because it spurs them to adopt new and better behaviors.
• Tell the story! When creating direct experiences is difficult, using a meaningful and persuasive story can be a powerful method to influence behavior change. Storytelling helps awaken people to the consequences of their actions.
• Game: another crucial way to make people love a behavior they hate is by turning it into something that feels like a game. People tend to enjoy any activity that involves clear goals and feedback.

People will naturally carry out tasks without being told to do so.

Personal ability involves influencing people to do what they believe they can't do. To achieve this, you must demand full attention from the people you're trying to influence. Spend more time engaging your audience in practicing what you have taught them, and then ensure you give them constant feedback. Also, you can break the vital behaviors into smaller actions such that it becomes easy for people to track their progress. Finally, have a deliberate practice routine that will help people overcome setbacks.

Hard work pays off in the future. Laziness pays off now. ~ Steven Wright

chapters.title: Use social motivation and social ability to drive change among individuals in your circle
chapters.text:
The best way to influence others to adopt difficult or uncommon behaviors is to practice those behaviors; you can’t just tell people about the new behavior; they have to see you live by it. Generally, people won't even trust what you're saying if you're unwilling to sacrifice your old values for new ones; they need to see you surrender important things like money, time, and ego before following you. As you influence others, try to seek the support of individuals who have a relationship with the people you're trying to influence. These intermediaries will help encourage those you intend to influence to take action. Look for ways to partner with these important people, don't try to do it alone.

As you continue your influencing efforts, you might discover that you need to make certain changes in specific ways due to existing norms. When you’re proposing something different from what people are used to, the first thing to do is go public. Openly discuss these changes, then invite people to hold each other accountable for either encouraging the proper behavior or confronting the wrong attitudes.

Teamwork facilitates idea generation, creativity, improves productivity, and leads to better results.

But don't just stop at encouraging people to adopt vital behaviors; you need to provide social ability — putting measures in place for people to assist one another to achieve a particular goal. Most times, changing a vital behavior will require the assistance of others, and It would be wise to carry people along on the journey in such situations.

People find it easier to succeed when they get help from their friends who give access to new knowledge, share resources, and provide social capital to achieve their goals. According to author James Surowiecki, working in groups yields better results than doing things alone. So, one key way in influencing people is to bring them together to form a team and encourage them to assist each other in achieving their individual goals.

chapters.title: Using structural motivation and ability as the first influence strategy yields bad results
chapters.text:
Most leaders believe in using structural motivations like incentives, salaries, and bonuses to spur people's changes. Building a stable audience means you do not allow positive and negative incentives to undermine your ability to influence others. In the long run, most incentives tend to backfire because people see them as the first motivation strategy to impact others.

To motivate others, allow vital behaviors to connect intrinsically with people first, then create social support before thinking of incentives. Research conducted by Dr. Mark Lepper in 1973 revealed that rewarding people to engage in an activity they already enjoy tends to yield bad results. When the reward withdraws, people might do less of the activity; but this is not to say incentives are wrong. However, you have to learn to use them wisely and not allow them to take the place of personal and social motivation. Always ensure that it is the vital behaviors people are showing that you're rewarding, not the results.

Incentives work better when tailored to the needs of the audience.

Aside from structural motivation, structural abilities can be effective in influencing others. Structural ability involves taking advantage of the physical environment to help people adopt the right behaviors. Our surroundings affect many things we do, yet we ignore our environment. When we see people misbehaving, the first thing that often comes to our minds is to change them and not their environment. Little things like the impact of physical space and the things in it can be great sources of influence. According to criminologist George Kelling, a disordered environment can send an unspoken message that encourages unpleasant behaviors. To influence others, you need to learn to notice the impact people’s surroundings have on them to help you identify some environmental elements that are driving their behavior. Lastly, you can put cues in the environment that will remind people of the behavior you are trying to influence.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
You have all it takes to influence the behavior of others; just use the right tools and be willing to put in the necessary effort. To become an effective influencer in any niche, you must learn to focus and measure. First, state clearly what you want to achieve, then measure your goals frequently to track your progress. Additionally, ensure that you track the right things and focus on a specific behavior.

It’s essential to take your time to identify what you want, don't rush things. Don't waste your time searching for numerous behaviors that will point you in the wrong direction. Instead, focus on a few vital behaviors that will bring about your desired result. Lastly, as you create your influence strategy, use all the sources of influence: personal motivation and ability, social motivation, and structural motivation.

Impact is crucial in the workplace because it's the best way to help others achieve important company objectives. Influencing others helps you build strong relationships based on the trust your audience has in your methods. It also creates lasting impressions in the hearts of others. Positively influencing others can make the world a better place. But like all good things, influencing has its disadvantages. People count on the influencer to always be perfect because of their expectations and trust, so conflicts often arise when something isn’t right. It is best to remain transparent at every stage and convince the audience of your intentions.

The journey to becoming an influencer is not an easy one, but it is worth it in the end. Everyone can display better behaviors and make cooler choices — this is the power of influence.

Try this

If you live in a neighborhood where people do not communicate with each other, you can begin to look for ways to foster good relationships among them. You can start by being intentional about saying hello to them and being of help when you see that they are in need. This could influence others to do the same and become friendly neighbors.

Read it till the end?
